items.title: Education Department proposes rules for new Workforce Pell Grants (starts July 2026)
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education issued proposed rules to create Workforce Pell Grants, allowing Pell funding for short-term workforce programs of 150–599 clock hours that last at least 8 weeks but less than 15 weeks. Public comments are due by April 8, 2026, and the Department says students could begin using Workforce Pell starting July 2026.

items.title: MACPAC March 2026 report urges wage transparency to strengthen Medicaid HCBS workforce
items.summary: The Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission (MACPAC) released its March 2026 Report to Congress with a recommendation to require states to report hourly wages paid to certain home- and community-based services (HCBS) workers. MACPAC says better wage data can help states set more effective HCBS payment rates amid workforce shortages.

items.title: CMS: Medicaid expansion adults must renew eligibility every 6 months starting Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS issued guidance implementing a new requirement for most Medicaid expansion adults (including equivalent coverage under certain waivers) to complete eligibility redeterminations twice per year, beginning with renewals scheduled on or after January 1, 2027. CMS says states must submit a State Plan Amendment attesting to the change, and the SPA may be submitted no later than March 31, 2027.

items.title: Judge blocks USDA from withholding SNAP admin funding over states’ refusal to provide recipient data
items.summary: A federal judge issued a temporary injunction preventing USDA from cutting off administrative funding to 22 states unless they turn over detailed SNAP recipient data (including names, addresses, and immigration status). Reuters reported the court said USDA’s funding threats conflicted with the legal framework governing SNAP and raised concerns about unauthorized sharing of sensitive information.

items.title: USDA posts FY2026 SNAP COLA tables (max benefit $298 for 1 person in most states; minimum $24)
items.summary: USDA’s FY2026 SNAP cost-of-living adjustment tables show the maximum monthly allotment for a 1-person household in the 48 states and D.C. is $298, with a minimum monthly allotment of $24 for 1–2 person households. The FY2026 standards are effective October 1, 2025 through September 30, 2026, and also update deductions and income limits.

items.title: HUD changes EHV/Stability Voucher verification rules; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-05 removing a prior waiver that let PHAs admit Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher applicants without first verifying SSNs and eligible immigration status. PHAs must comply with standard SSN and citizenship/eligible immigration status verification rules, and HUD set a 60-day compliance deadline (by May 4, 2026) for new Stability Voucher admissions and current EHV/SV families.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD to recapture and reallocate unused Emergency Housing Voucher services fees to cover 2026 shortfalls
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-02 establishing a process to recapture undisbursed and unspent EHV services fees and reallocate them as housing assistance payments (HAP) to cover EHV shortfalls and extend assistance. HUD said it planned to send a final reconciliation/closeout enclosure to PHAs by the end of February 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD makes $10 million available in additional HUD-VASH admin fees; PHA applications due March 2, 2026
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-01 announcing $10 million in additional administrative fees for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH vouchers to support leasing and related administration. HUD said applications must be received by midnight on March 2, 2026 (local time for the PHA).
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD rescinds many COVID-era Housing Choice Voucher/Public Housing waivers and guidance (PIH 2026-06)
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-06 rescinding numerous CARES Act and COVID-era notices affecting Housing Choice Voucher and public housing operations, and partially rescinding portions of PIH 2021-14 related to HCV and public housing programs. HUD also revised PIH 2020-32 by removing certain limited-English-proficiency guidance related to remote hearings and briefings.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: IRS: Most EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2, 2026; 'Where’s My Refund?' updates by Feb. 21
items.summary: The IRS said it expects most refunds for Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) filers who use direct deposit to be available by March 2, 2026, assuming there are no other issues with the return. The IRS also said 'Where’s My Refund?' should show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: USDA: WIC food package changes must be implemented within 24 months; some items sooner
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Q&A says state agencies must implement the final WIC food package revisions within 24 months of the final rule’s publication date, with exceptions. USDA says increased fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit (CVB) amounts must be implemented no later than 60 days after publication, and yogurt vitamin D requirements (for states that authorize yogurt) must be implemented within 36 months; the page cites FY2024 CVB amounts of $26 (children), $47 (pregnant/postpartum), and $52 (mostly/fully breastfeeding).

items.title: SSA: 2026 Social Security and SSI COLA set at 2.8% (SSI federal payment standard $994/month for individuals)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 and shows the SSI federal payment standard rising to $994/month for an eligible individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple. The fact sheet also lists the 2026 Social Security taxable earnings maximum as $184,500.

items.title: SSA updates 2026 disability work thresholds (SGA $1,690/month; trial work period $1,210/month)
items.summary: SSA’s Red Book 'What’s New in 2026' lists the Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) level at $1,690/month for non-blind individuals and $2,830/month for blind individuals. It also lists the trial work period (TWP) monthly amount at $1,210 and the SSI student earned income exclusion at $2,410/month (up to $9,730/year).

items.title: KFF outlines Medicaid policy issues to watch in 2026, including provider tax changes and work requirement timelines
items.summary: KFF’s 2026 outlook says states face fiscal pressure as they implement Medicaid changes from the 2025 reconciliation law, including new federal work requirements starting January 1, 2027 and restrictions on provider taxes, with some 'uniformity waiver' provider tax revisions as early as April 1, 2026. KFF also cites estimates that Medicaid policy changes would increase the uninsured by 7.5 million by 2034, with 5.3 million tied to work requirements.
